steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses and the connector
steps.content: The sentence contains two complete thoughts: “The sand is a more unusual shade” and “deposits of crushed coral lend the sand a unique pink hue.” The word “though” must connect these as a transitional element signaling an explanatory relationship between them.

steps.title: Apply the transitional word punctuation rule
steps.content:
When though connects two independent clauses as a transitional element, place a comma before it and a semicolon after: IC, though; IC. The comma marks the end of the first clause, and the semicolon separates the transitional word from the beginning of the next complete thought.

Options B and C fail to include the semicolon after though, and option D incorrectly places the semicolon before though instead of after.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
